title: Java Properties
documentation:
## Purpose
Represents a Java `.properties` file, a flat key-value configuration format used throughout the Java ecosystem for application settings, internationalisation strings, and framework configuration.

## When to use
Use `media:properties;textable;record` when a node produces or consumes Java properties files, such as Spring Boot application properties or resource bundle files. For non-Java key-value configurations, see [INI Configuration](https://capdag.com/browse/media:ini;textable;record).

## Notes
Keys and values are separated by `=` or `:`. Lines beginning with `#` or `!` are comments. Values may use Unicode escape sequences (`\\uXXXX`). The encoding is ISO-8859-1 by default, though UTF-8 is common in modern tooling.
